member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.AlertsController.GetAll(System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Retrieve all alerts with optional filtering by disaster ID. Open to all users. Returns most recent alerts first.
member.remarks:
Alerts are automatically generated when: - A new disaster report is submitted - A disaster is verified by Admin - A responder assignment status is updated
Severity values: Low, Medium, High, Critical. Audience values: Public (citizens), Responders, Admin.
member.param: Optional: Filter alerts by specific disaster ID. If omitted, returns all alerts.
member.returns: List of alerts with ID, message, severity, audience, and timestamp

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.AssignmentsController.GetByDisaster(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieve all responder assignments for a specific disaster. Open to all users. Shows which organizations have been assigned and their current status.
member.remarks: A disaster typically has one primary assignment (auto-assigned nearest responder). Additional assignments may exist if Admin creates manual overrides or adds backup responders. Status values: Assigned, EnRoute, OnSite, Completed, Failed.
member.returns: List of assignments for the disaster with organization details and status

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.AssignmentsController.OverrideAssignment(System.Int32,DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.DTOs.OverrideAssignmentDto)
member.summary: Manually override a disaster assignment to a different responder organization. Restricted to Admin role. Used when auto-assignment is inappropriate or responder capacity is exceeded.
member.remarks: Admin can reassign a disaster to any active responder organization. Previous assignment is not deleted—a new assignment record is created with method = "AdminOverride". New organization is notified in real-time via SignalR.
member.param: The existing assignment ID to override
member.returns: Updated assignment with new organization details

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.AssignmentsController.UpdateAssignmentStatus(System.Int32,DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.DTOs.UpdateAssignmentStatusDto)
member.summary: Update the current status of a responder assignment. Restricted to Admin and Responder roles. Used by responder organizations to report progress.
member.remarks: Typical progression: Assigned → EnRoute → OnSite → Completed. A "Failed" status indicates the responder cannot complete the assignment (e.g., access denied, resource unavailable). All status updates trigger real-time alert notifications to Admin and Citizens.
member.param: New assignment status (Assigned, EnRoute, OnSite, Completed, Failed)
member.returns: Updated assignment with new status

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.AuthController.Register(DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.DTOs.RegisterDto)
member.summary: Register a new user account with a specified role. Open endpoint—citizens can self-register. Only existing Admins can create other Admins.
member.remarks: Available roles: Citizen, Responder, Admin. Responders must specify their organization ID (Rescue 1122, Edhi, PDMA, etc.). Email must be unique. Password must be at least 6 characters and include at least one non-alphanumeric character.
member.param: Registration data: FullName, Email, Password, Role, ResponderOrganizationId (if Responder)
member.returns: JWT token, user ID, role, and token expiration timestamp

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.AuthController.Login(DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.DTOs.LoginDto)
member.summary: Authenticate a user and issue a JWT token. Open endpoint—all users (Citizen, Responder, Admin) can log in with email and password.
member.remarks: Returns a JWT token valid for 24 hours. Token must be included in subsequent requests as: Authorization: Bearer {token}
member.returns: JWT token, user ID, full name, role, organization (if Responder), and token expiration

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.AuthController.Me
member.summary: Retrieve the currently logged-in user's profile information. Restricted to authenticated users (requires valid JWT token).
member.remarks: Used by frontends to populate user profile, display role, verify authentication status. Token expiration is returned as zero since this endpoint does not issue a new token.
member.returns: Current user's ID, full name, email, role, and organization (if applicable)

member.name: T:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.DisastersController
member.summary: Controller that exposes disaster-related endpoints (list, get, create, verify and update status). Handles verification workflow and triggers responder assignment and alerts.
member.remarks: Route: api/disasters Roles: Admin and Responder for state-changing endpoints; read endpoints are public.

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.DisastersController.GetAll(System.Nullable{DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.Entity.Enums.DisasterStatus})
member.summary: Retrieve all disasters with optional filtering by status. Open to all users. Returns most recent disasters first.
member.remarks: Status values: Reported, Verified, ResponseInProgress, Resolved, FalseAlarm. If no status filter is provided, returns all disasters. Used for real-time dashboard displays and citizen alerts.
member.returns: List of disasters matching the filter, sorted by most recent first

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.DisastersController.GetById(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieve a specific disaster by ID. Open to all users. Includes location, severity, type, and verification details.
member.returns: Disaster details (location, type, severity, status, verified timestamp)

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.DisastersController.AdminCreate(DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.DTOs.CreateAdminDisasterDto)
member.summary: Manually create a new disaster record. Restricted to Admin role. Rare—most disasters originate from citizen reports. Used when disasters are detected by external APIs (weather, earthquake) or entered directly by NDMA.
member.remarks: Disaster begins in "Reported" status. After creation, Admin must call /verify to trigger responder assignment. Location (latitude, longitude) must be within Pakistan range (Lat: 23–37, Lon: 60–77).
member.param: Disaster details (type, severity, location, description, affected area radius)
member.returns: The newly created disaster record

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.DisastersController.VerifyDisaster(System.Int32)
member.summary: Verify a disaster and trigger automatic assignment of the nearest responder organization. Restricted to Admin role. This is a critical trigger point in the response workflow.
member.remarks:
When a disaster is verified: 1. Status changes to "Verified" 2. Nearest responder organization is auto-assigned by distance 3. Real-time alert is broadcast via SignalR to all connected citizens and responders 4. Assignment record is created with method = "AutoAssigned"
Disaster must be in "Reported" status to be verified.
member.returns: Verified disaster, assignment ID, assigned organization ID, and assignment method

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.DisastersController.UpdateStatus(System.Int32,DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.DTOs.UpdateDisasterStatusDto)
member.summary: Update the status of an ongoing disaster. Restricted to Admin and Responder roles. Used to track disaster progression and resolution.
member.remarks: Common status transitions: Reported → Verified (by /verify endpoint) Verified → ResponseInProgress (by responder org) ResponseInProgress → Resolved (when responder completes) Any Status → FalseAlarm (if confirmed to be invalid)
member.returns: Updated disaster details with new status

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.GeocodingController.Search(System.String)
member.summary: Search for a Pakistan location by name. Returns lat/lon automatically. Use this before submitting a report — user types area name, you get coordinates.
member.remarks: Examples: "DHA Lahore", "Gulshan Karachi", "F-7 Islamabad", "Saddar Peshawar"

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.GeocodingController.Reverse(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Get location name from lat/lon (reverse geocoding). Useful for displaying human-readable location on disaster cards.

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.OrganizationsController.GetAll(System.Nullable{DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.Entity.Enums.OrganizationType},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Retrieve all responder organizations with optional filtering. Open to all users. Essential for assigning disasters and viewing responder capacity.
member.remarks: Organization types: Rescue1122, Edhi, PDMA, FireDepartment, PoliceRescue, Military, HealthServices, Other. By default, returns only active organizations. Set activeOnly=false to include deactivated organizations. Each organization has a base location (latitude, longitude) used for distance-based auto-assignment.
member.param: Optional: Filter organizations by type (Rescue1122, Edhi, PDMA, etc.)
Filter to active organizations only (default: true)
member.returns: List of organizations matching the filters with contact info and base location

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.OrganizationsController.GetById(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieve a specific responder organization by ID. Open to all users. Includes organization type, contact details, and base location.
member.returns: Organization details (name, type, contact number, base coordinates, active status)

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.OrganizationsController.Create(DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.DTOs.CreateOrganizationDto)
member.summary: Register a new responder organization. Restricted to Admin role. Used to onboard new rescue/response entities (e.g., a new district PDMA branch).
member.remarks: Organization type must be one of: Rescue1122, Edhi, PDMA, FireDepartment, PoliceRescue, Military, HealthServices, Other. Base latitude and longitude (organization headquarters) are critical for auto-assignment distance calculations. All new organizations are created in "active" status.
member.param: Organization data (name, type, contact number, base location)
member.returns: The newly created organization record

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.OrganizationsController.Update(System.Int32,DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.DTOs.UpdateOrganizationDto)
member.summary: Update an existing responder organization's details. Restricted to Admin role. Used to modify contact info, base location, or activation status.
member.remarks: All fields can be updated: name, contact number, base location, and active status. Changing base location affects future auto-assignment distance calculations for new disasters. Note: Organization type cannot be changed after creation.
member.returns: Updated organization record

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.OrganizationsController.Deactivate(System.Int32)
member.summary: Deactivate a responder organization (soft delete). Restricted to Admin role. Organization records and assignment history are preserved for audit purposes.
member.remarks: Soft delete: organization is marked inactive but not permanently removed. Deactivated organizations will not appear in active-only queries and cannot be assigned new disasters. Existing assignments to deactivated organizations remain in the database for historical reference. To restore, Admin must manually reactivate via the Update endpoint.
member.returns: No content (204) on success

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.ReportsController.Create(DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.DTOs.CreateReportDto)
member.summary: Submit a new disaster report. Any logged-in user (Citizen, Responder, or Admin) can submit. Admin is notified in real-time via SignalR.
member.remarks: Latitude must be in range 23–37 (Pakistan range). Longitude must be in range 60–77. Report status begins as "Pending" and requires Admin verification.
member.param: The disaster report details (latitude, longitude, description, etc.)
member.returns: Created report with ID and timestamps

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.ReportsController.GetById(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieve a specific disaster report by ID.
member.returns: Report details including location, description, and status

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.ReportsController.GetAll(System.Nullable{DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.Entity.Enums.ReportStatus})
member.summary: Get all disaster reports with optional filtering by status. Restricted to Admin role. Used to review pending reports and manage verification workflow.
member.remarks: Status values: Pending, Verified, Rejected, Merged. If no status filter is provided, returns all reports sorted by most recent first.
member.param: Optional: Filter reports by status (Pending, Verified, Rejected, Merged)
member.returns: List of reports matching the filter criteria

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.ReportsController.GetMyReports
member.summary: Get reports created by the currently logged-in user.

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.ReportsController.GetNearby(System.Double,System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Find pending disaster reports near a given location. Restricted to Admin role. Used to detect potential duplicate reports before merging.
member.remarks: Returns all pending reports within the specified radius (in kilometers) of the given coordinates. Helps Admin identify related incidents that should be merged into a single disaster.
member.returns: List of nearby pending reports within the radius

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.ReportsController.MergeExisting(System.Int32,DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.DTOs.MergeIntoExistingDto)
member.summary: Link a pending report to an existing verified disaster. Restricted to Admin role. Use when a report describes an already-tracked disaster.
member.remarks: This consolidates related reports into one disaster record. The report status changes to "Merged" and responders are not re-assigned.
member.param: Contains the target disaster ID to merge into
member.returns: The existing disaster details after merge

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.ReportsController.CreateDisasterFromReport(System.Int32,DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.DTOs.CreateDisasterFromReportDto)
member.summary: Create a new disaster record from a pending report. Restricted to Admin role. Use when a report describes a NEW disaster event.
member.remarks: Transforms a pending report into a verified disaster and automatically assigns the nearest responder organization. Broadcasts real-time alert to all connected clients via SignalR. The disaster begins in "Verified" state and immediately triggers responder assignment.
member.param: The pending report ID to convert
member.returns: The newly created disaster and auto-assigned responder

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.ReportsController.Reject(System.Int32,DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Core.DTOs.RejectReportDto)
member.summary: Reject a pending report with a reason. Restricted to Admin role. Use when a report is invalid, false alarm, or duplicate.
member.remarks: Report status changes to "Rejected". The rejector's reason is recorded for audit purposes. No responders are assigned when a report is rejected.
member.param: The pending report ID to reject
member.returns: The rejected report details

member.name: M:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Controllers.WeatherForecastController.Get
member.summary: Retrieve a sample 5-day weather forecast (template endpoint). Open to all users. This is a demo/test endpoint.
member.remarks: This endpoint returns randomly generated forecast data for demonstration purposes. In production, integrate with weather.gov.pk, weather APIs, or other meteorological services to provide real weather forecasts for disaster risk assessment.
member.returns: Array of 5 weather forecast entries with date, temperature, and conditions

member.name: T:DisasterPreparedness_ResponseSystem.Swagger.EnumSchemaFilter
member.summary: SchemaFilter to ensure enum values are displayed as strings (not numeric values) in Swagger/OpenAPI documentation. This filter converts enum schemas to display human-readable names like "Verified", "Pending", etc. instead of numeric values.
